paper_title: Analytical Methods and Applications in Mineral Processing

methods.description: Investigation of the association between carbonates and diatom frustules, as well as variations in diatom morphology and preservation using ESEM.

steps.description: The observations were conducted on rock chips to avoid any alteration of the diatom frustules induced by sample preparation.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct ESEM observations on rock chips

steps.description: The instrument used was a Philips XL30 ESEM and supported with element analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform element analysis using Philips XL30 ESEM

methods.description: Determination of the amount of CaCO3 and the concentration of major elements in the samples to support mineralogical analyses using XRF.

steps.description: The amount of CaCO3 and the concentration of major elements in the samples were determined using XRF.
steps.searchable_summary: determine CaCO3 and major elements using XRF

methods.description: Identification of mineralogical content of samples using IR spectroscopy, particularly for detecting amorphous phases in minerals.

steps.description: The samples used for all the spectral analyses were gently powdered using pestle and mortar, trying to preserve the delicate diatom structure, and later homogenised.
steps.searchable_summary: powder and homogenize samples for IR spectroscopy

steps.description: Laboratory data were collected using attenuated total reflection (ATR), with an ATR-Diamond accessory in a Perkin Elmer Spectrum 100 FTIR spectrometer.

methods.description: Processing of spectral data collected from various spectrometers to assess measuring and analyzing conditions for on-site applications.

steps.description: Bidirectional reflectance spectra were collected using a PANalytical ASD FieldSpec spectrometer.
Diffuse reflectance spectra were measured with an Agilent 4300 hand-held FTIR spectrometer.
The spectral files were processed in the R environment. First, noise reduction was conducted by averaging the spectra recorded for every sample.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce noise in spectra using averaging in R

steps.description: The bidirectional reflectance spectra were processed using continuum removal and derivatives.
steps.searchable_summary: process bidirectional spectra with continuum removal

steps.description: The diffuse reflectance spectra were smoothed using the Savitzky-Golay filter with polynomial order of 3 and window size of 55 data points.
steps.searchable_summary: smooth diffuse spectra using Savitzky-Golay filter

steps.description: Further principal component analysis (PCA) was also performed in the R environment using the Chemometrics With R package.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on spectral data in R

methods.description: Characterization of diatomite using infrared spectroscopy to identify calcite and aragonite as main ore impurities and establish correspondence with ore quality.

steps.description: The identification of the type of carbonate was possible by using a portable instrument that uses diffuse reflectance spectroscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: identify carbonate type using portable diffuse reflectance spectroscopy

steps.description: It was feasible to perform a qualitative analysis using principal component analysis (PCA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform qualitative PCA on carbonate spectra
